title: Food Safety & Handling Procedure
description:
Comprehensive procedure for ensuring food safety across all hotel F&B operations. Covers hand hygiene, temperature monitoring, cross-contamination prevention, and corrective actions for temperature deviations.

All staff involved in food preparation, storage, or service must follow this SOP without exception.

steps.title: Hand Washing Protocol
steps.description:
All staff must wash hands thoroughly before handling food:

  • Wet hands with warm running water
  • Apply antibacterial soap
  • Scrub all surfaces for at least 20 seconds
  • Rinse under clean running water
  • Dry with single-use paper towel

steps.title: Temperature Check
steps.description: Verify storage temperatures are within safe ranges using calibrated thermometers.
checklist_items: Verify thermometer calibration date is current

steps.title: Temperature Out of Range?
steps.description: If any temperature reading is outside the acceptable range, immediate action is required. Evaluate the situation and follow the appropriate branch.

steps.title: Corrective Action for Temperature Deviation
steps.description:
Immediate actions required:

  1. Move affected food items to a compliant storage unit
  2. Check equipment for malfunction
  3. Contact maintenance if equipment is faulty
  4. Document the deviation in the incident log
  5. Discard any food that has been in the danger zone (4°C–60°C) for more than 2 hours

steps.title: Cross-Contamination Prevention
steps.description:
Prevent cross-contamination by following these guidelines:

  • Use separate cutting boards for raw meat, vegetables, and ready-to-eat foods
  • Store raw meats on the lowest shelf
  • Use colour-coded utensils as per the kitchen chart
  • Clean and sanitise surfaces between tasks

steps.title: End-of-Shift Verification
steps.description: Before ending your shift, confirm all food safety protocols have been followed. Sign off on the daily food safety checklist and hand over to the next shift lead.
checklist_items: Food items properly stored and labelled
Equipment turned off or set to overnight mode

versions.change_summary: Major revision: added decision branching for temperature alerts
Updated temperature thresholds and added photo requirements

title: Customer Complaint Resolution
description: Step-by-step guide for handling customer complaints from initial receipt through resolution and follow-up. Ensures consistent service recovery across all properties.

steps.title: Acknowledge the Complaint
steps.description: Listen actively to the guest. Make eye contact, nod, and avoid interrupting. Use empathetic language such as "I understand how frustrating this must be."

steps.title: Document the Issue
steps.description: Record all relevant details in the complaint management system.

steps.title: Determine Severity
steps.description: Assess the severity of the complaint to determine the appropriate response level and escalation path.

decision_branches.label: Minor — Can be resolved on the spot

steps.title: On-the-Spot Resolution
steps.description:
Resolve minor complaints immediately:

  • Offer a sincere apology
  • Provide an appropriate remedy (room change, complimentary item, etc.)
  • Confirm the guest is satisfied with the resolution
  • Document the resolution in the system

steps.title: Follow-Up Within 24 Hours
steps.description: Contact the guest within 24 hours to ensure satisfaction. Send a follow-up email or make a courtesy call. Update the complaint record with the outcome.

versions.change_summary: Added follow-up timeline and documentation requirements

title: Daily Opening & Closing Checklist
description: Comprehensive checklist for property opening and closing procedures. Covers security, equipment checks, facility readiness, and end-of-day reconciliation.

steps.title: Morning Security Check
steps.description: Walk the perimeter and verify all entry points are secure. Check CCTV systems are operational.
checklist_items: All exterior doors locked/unlocked as scheduled

steps.title: Facility Systems Startup
steps.description: Activate all facility systems in the correct sequence to avoid electrical surges.

steps.title: Staff Briefing
steps.description:
Conduct a 10-minute morning briefing covering:

  • Occupancy status and VIP arrivals
  • Special events or group bookings
  • Maintenance issues from overnight
  • Staff assignments for the day

steps.title: Public Area Readiness
steps.description: Inspect all public areas before guest access.

steps.title: Evening Cash Reconciliation
steps.description: Complete all financial reconciliation before locking up.

steps.title: Closing Security Sweep
steps.description: Perform a full security sweep before locking the property.

steps.title: Overnight Notes
steps.description: Document any issues or notes for the morning shift in the handover log.

steps.title: Key Management
steps.description: Warning: All master keys must be returned to the secure key cabinet. Never leave keys unattended. Report any missing keys immediately to security.

versions.change_summary: Added evening procedures and key management

title: Fire Evacuation Procedure
description: Emergency fire evacuation procedure for all properties. Defines roles, assembly points, and communication protocols during fire emergencies.

steps.title: Activate Fire Alarm
steps.description: CRITICAL: Upon discovering a fire, immediately activate the nearest fire alarm pull station. Do not attempt to fight the fire unless it is small and contained.

steps.title: Call Emergency Services
steps.description: Call 911 (or local emergency number) immediately. Provide: location, nature of fire, number of occupants, any known injuries.

steps.title: Evacuation Procedures
steps.description: Guide all occupants to the nearest exit. Use stairs only — never elevators. Assist guests with mobility issues. Close doors behind you to slow fire spread.

steps.title: Assembly Point Check-In
steps.description: Report to the designated assembly point and verify all staff and guests are accounted for.
checklist_items: Missing persons reported to fire department

steps.title: Post-Incident Documentation
steps.description: Complete the fire incident report within 2 hours. Include timeline, actions taken, and any injuries or property damage.

versions.change_summary: Updated assembly points and added multi-property support

title: Housekeeping Room Turnover
description: Standard procedure for room cleaning and turnover between guest stays. Includes cleaning protocols, inspection points, and quality standards.

steps.title: Room Entry Protocol
steps.description: Knock three times, announce "Housekeeping," wait 10 seconds. If no response, use key card to enter. Place door wedge for safety.

steps.title: Strip and Replace Linens
steps.description: Remove all used linens. Inspect mattress pad for stains. Replace with fresh linens following the bed-making standard.

steps.title: Bathroom Deep Clean
steps.description: Complete bathroom cleaning following the sanitisation checklist.

steps.title: Room Inspection
steps.description: Final quality inspection before marking room as ready.

versions.change_summary: Updated cleaning standards and added inspection checklist

title: Guest Check-In Process
description: Standard operating procedure for guest check-in at front desk. Covers identity verification, payment processing, room assignment, and welcome orientation.

steps.title: Greet the Guest
steps.description: Welcome the guest with a warm smile. Use their name if visible on the reservation. "Welcome to [Hotel Name], [Guest Name]. We're delighted to have you."

steps.title: Verify Identity & Reservation
steps.description: Confirm the reservation and verify guest identity.

steps.title: Room Assignment & Key Issue
steps.description: Assign the room, program key cards, and provide the guest with essential information about their stay.

steps.title: Facility Orientation
steps.description: Brief the guest on hotel facilities, breakfast times, WiFi access, and emergency exits. Offer to assist with luggage.

versions.change_summary: Updated welcome script and added facility orientation

title: HVAC Maintenance Schedule
description: Preventive maintenance schedule and procedures for all HVAC systems. Covers filter replacement, coil cleaning, refrigerant checks, and seasonal adjustments.

steps.title: Monthly Filter Inspection
steps.description: Inspect all air filters monthly. Replace filters that show visible dirt accumulation or have been in service for 90+ days.

steps.title: Quarterly Coil Cleaning
steps.description: Clean evaporator and condenser coils quarterly. Use approved coil cleaning solution. Rinse thoroughly and allow to dry before restarting unit.

steps.title: Refrigerant Level Check
steps.description: Caution: Refrigerant handling must be performed by certified technicians only. Check pressure readings against manufacturer specifications.

steps.title: Documentation
steps.description: Record all maintenance activities in the HVAC maintenance log. Include date, technician, work performed, and any parts replaced.

title: Data Privacy & GDPR Compliance
description: Procedure for handling personal data in compliance with GDPR and local data protection regulations. Covers data collection, storage, access rights, and breach notification.

steps.title: Data Collection Consent
steps.description: Obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data. Present the privacy notice and obtain signature or digital consent.

steps.title: Data Storage Requirements
steps.description: Ensure all personal data is stored in approved systems with appropriate encryption.
checklist_items: Data stored in approved system only

steps.title: Data Subject Access Request
steps.description: When a guest requests access to their data, respond within 30 days. Verify identity before releasing any information.

steps.title: Data Breach Protocol
steps.description: CRITICAL: In the event of a data breach, notify the Data Protection Officer within 4 hours. The DPO must notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach.

steps.title: Reference: Data Classification Guide
steps.description: Refer to the Data Classification Guide (Document DC-001) for categories of personal data and corresponding handling requirements.

title: Employee Onboarding Procedure
description: Comprehensive onboarding process for new employees from offer acceptance through the first 90 days. Covers documentation, training, system access, and performance checkpoints.

steps.title: Pre-Arrival Documentation
steps.description: Ensure all required documents are collected before the start date.

steps.title: Day 1 Orientation
steps.description: Welcome the new employee, provide facility tour, introduce to team members, and review company policies and values.

steps.title: System Access Setup
steps.description: Configure all necessary system accounts and access permissions.

steps.title: 30-Day Check-In
steps.description: Schedule and conduct a 30-day performance check-in. Review progress, address concerns, and adjust training plan if needed.

steps.title: 90-Day Review
steps.description: Formal 90-day performance review. Confirm probation completion or extend if necessary. Set goals for the next quarter.

title: Workplace Harassment Prevention
description: Policy and procedures for preventing, identifying, and reporting workplace harassment. Defines unacceptable behavior, reporting channels, and investigation process.

steps.title: Understanding Harassment
steps.description: Harassment includes any unwelcome conduct based on race, gender, religion, age, disability, or sexual orientation that creates a hostile work environment. This includes verbal, physical, and visual harassment.

steps.title: Reporting an Incident
steps.description:
If you experience or witness harassment, report it immediately through any of the following channels:

  • Direct supervisor (if not the harasser)
  • HR department
  • Anonymous hotline: 1-800-REPORT
  • Online reporting portal

steps.title: Investigation Process
steps.description: All reports are investigated within 5 business days. The investigation includes interviews with all parties, review of evidence, and a written determination.

title: Allergen Management Protocol
description: Procedures for managing food allergens in F&B operations. Covers allergen identification, menu labelling, cross-contact prevention, and emergency response for allergic reactions.

steps.title: Allergen Identification
steps.description: Know the 14 major allergens: cereals containing gluten, crustaceans, eggs, fish, peanuts, soybeans, milk, nuts, celery, mustard, sesame, sulphites, lupin, and molluscs.

steps.title: Guest Allergen Inquiry
steps.description: Always ask guests about allergies before taking food orders. Document allergies on the order ticket with a red allergen flag.

steps.title: Kitchen Allergen Protocols
steps.description: Follow strict allergen-safe preparation procedures.
checklist_items: Dish marked with allergen flag before service

steps.title: Allergic Reaction Response
steps.description: EMERGENCY: If a guest shows signs of an allergic reaction (difficulty breathing, swelling, hives), call emergency services immediately. Locate the nearest EpiPen in the first aid station.

versions.change_summary: Updated allergen list and emergency response protocol

title: Revenue Management Pricing Strategy
description: Guidelines for dynamic pricing, rate adjustments, and revenue optimization across all booking channels.

steps.title: Daily Rate Review
steps.description: Review competitor rates and occupancy forecasts every morning. Adjust pricing within the approved range for the current demand level.

steps.title: Demand Assessment
steps.description: Evaluate current and forecasted demand to determine pricing tier.

steps.title: High Demand Pricing
steps.description: Apply premium rates. Close discount channels. Implement minimum stay requirements for peak dates.

steps.title: Low Demand Strategy
steps.description: Activate promotional rates on OTAs. Enable corporate and group discounts. Consider package deals with F&B and spa services.

steps.title: Weekly Revenue Report
steps.description: Generate and review the weekly revenue report. Compare RevPAR, ADR, and occupancy against budget and same period last year.

title: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Compliance
description: Procedures for identifying and reporting suspicious transactions in compliance with anti-money laundering regulations. Covers customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, and SAR filing.

steps.title: Customer Due Diligence
steps.description: Verify the identity of guests making cash transactions exceeding $3,000. Collect and record identification documents.

steps.title: Transaction Monitoring
steps.description:
Monitor for suspicious transaction patterns including:

  • Multiple cash payments just below reporting threshold
  • Unusual third-party payments
  • Requests to break large bills without a purchase

steps.title: Suspicious Activity Report
steps.description: If suspicious activity is identified, file a SAR within 24 hours. Do not inform the subject of the report. Contact the compliance officer for guidance.

steps.title: Record Retention
steps.description: Maintain all transaction records and customer identification documents for a minimum of 5 years. Store securely with restricted access.

title: Sustainability & Waste Management
description: Environmental sustainability procedures including waste sorting, recycling protocols, energy conservation, and water management practices.

steps.title: Waste Sorting Guidelines
steps.description: Sort all waste into four categories: general waste (black bin), recyclables (blue bin), organic/food waste (green bin), and hazardous waste (red bin).

steps.title: Energy Conservation Practices
steps.description: Follow these daily energy conservation practices.
checklist_items: Turn off lights in unoccupied areas
Set HVAC to energy-saving mode after hours
Ensure windows/doors are closed when HVAC is running
Report any energy waste to maintenance

steps.title: Water Conservation
steps.description: Report any dripping taps or running toilets immediately. Use water-efficient cleaning methods. Pool backwash water must be properly drained to approved outlets.

title: Elevator Inspection & Emergency Procedure
description: Procedures for routine elevator inspections and emergency protocols when guests or staff are trapped in an elevator.

steps.title: Weekly Inspection Checklist
steps.description: Perform a visual and functional inspection of all elevators weekly.
checklist_items: Lighting in cabin and shaft adequate

steps.title: Entrapment Emergency
steps.description: If someone is trapped: Reassure them via the emergency intercom. Contact the elevator service company immediately. Do not attempt to manually open doors unless trained and authorised.

steps.title: Post-Incident Report
steps.description: Document every elevator incident. Include timeline, cause (if known), resolution, and any guest impact. Submit report to management within 24 hours.

title: Night Audit Procedure
description: Step-by-step night audit process for end-of-day financial reconciliation, system rollover, and preparation of daily reports.

steps.title: Pre-Audit Preparation
steps.description: Before starting the night audit, ensure all outlets have closed their shifts and submitted their reports.

steps.title: Revenue Posting Verification
steps.description: Verify all revenue postings are accurate. Check room rates against reservations. Investigate any rate discrepancies.

steps.title: System Date Rollover
steps.description: Critical step: Only perform the system date rollover after all verifications are complete. This action cannot be reversed.

steps.title: Generate Reports
steps.description: Generate and distribute the following reports.

steps.title: Night Audit Handover
steps.description: Complete the night audit log with notes for the morning shift. Flag any issues requiring immediate attention.

versions.change_summary: Draft: restructured for new PMS system

title: Chemical Safety & COSHH Handling
description: Procedures for the safe handling, storage, and disposal of chemicals and hazardous substances under COSHH regulations. Includes PPE requirements and spill response.

steps.title: PPE Requirements
steps.description: Always wear the required Personal Protective Equipment before handling any chemicals. Check the COSHH data sheet for specific PPE requirements.
checklist_items: Respiratory protection (if required by data sheet)

steps.title: Chemical Storage
steps.description: Store chemicals in designated locked cabinets. Never mix different chemicals. Ensure all containers are properly labelled with contents and hazard symbols.

steps.title: Spill Response
steps.description: In case of chemical spill: Evacuate the immediate area. Don appropriate PPE. Contain the spill using the spill kit. Do not wash chemicals into drains. Report to management immediately.

steps.title: Disposal Procedures
steps.description: Dispose of chemicals according to local environmental regulations. Never pour chemicals down drains. Use approved waste disposal contractors for hazardous materials.

versions.change_summary: Updated PPE requirements and added spill kit locations

title: VIP Guest Services Protocol
description: Premium service procedures for VIP guests including pre-arrival preparation, personalized amenities, priority handling, and post-departure follow-up.

steps.title: Pre-Arrival Preparation
steps.description: Review VIP guest preferences 48 hours before arrival. Prepare personalized welcome amenities based on guest history and preferences.
checklist_items: Room pre-inspected and upgraded if available

steps.title: Express Check-In
steps.description: VIP guests receive express check-in at the dedicated VIP desk or in-room. Pre-program key cards and have registration forms ready for signature.

steps.title: During-Stay Touch Points
steps.description: Make at least two proactive service contacts during the stay: one within 2 hours of check-in and one midway through the stay.

steps.title: Post-Departure Follow-Up
steps.description: Send a personalized thank-you email within 24 hours of departure. Update the guest preference database with any new insights from the stay.

title: Electrical Safety & Lockout/Tagout
description: Archived procedure for electrical safety. Replaced by updated version in compliance with new national electrical standards.

steps.title: Pre-Work Electrical Assessment
steps.description: Before any electrical work, verify circuits are de-energized. Use a voltage tester to confirm zero energy state.

steps.title: Lockout/Tagout Application
steps.description: Apply lockout devices and tags to all energy isolation points. Each worker applies their own lock. Never remove another person's lock.

steps.title: Work Completion & Restoration
steps.description: After work is complete, remove all tools, verify the area is clear, then remove lockout devices in reverse order. Re-energize and test.

title: Event Setup & Coordination
description: Standard procedures for setting up and coordinating events in hotel conference rooms and banquet facilities. Covers room configuration, AV setup, catering coordination, and post-event teardown.

steps.title: Pre-Event Planning Review
steps.description: Review the event BEO (Banquet Event Order) 48 hours in advance. Confirm room layout, AV requirements, catering menu, and special requests with the event coordinator.

steps.title: Room Configuration
steps.description: Set up the room according to the specified layout diagram.

steps.title: AV & Technology Setup
steps.description: Install and test all audio-visual equipment. Run a complete system check at least 1 hour before the event.

steps.title: Post-Event Teardown
steps.description: Complete teardown within 2 hours of event end. Return all equipment, clear catering items, and restore room to default configuration.

versions.change_summary: Added AV checklist and teardown timeline
